title: Service coverage & incident notification process
preview:
From: Alan Kerr <alan.kerr@firstnational.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, procurement@firstnational.com
Date: 2027-07-14T09:08:00-07:00
Subject: Service coverage & incident notification process

Ben —

Thanks for taking this RFI. For our procurement packet we need a short, specific response on the following items related to TAM/support: 1) standard TAM coverage hours and whether 24/7 on-call is available; 2) time-to-response SLAs by severity for both hosted API and Dedicated clusters; 3) escalation path and names/roles we would contact at each stage (phone + email); 4) example incident notification timeline and templated customer communications; 5) evidence for SOC2 / audit logging and where to retrieve it.

Can you send a single consolidated doc or point me to the location where we can retrieve those artifacts? Procurement timeline is tight — we need this by EOD Jul 15.

Alan Kerr
Sourcing Manager, First National Bank
alan.kerr@firstnational.com
procurement@firstnational.com

---
(Attachment reference: none in this initial email)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alan Kerr <alan.kerr@firstnational.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-07-14T10:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Service coverage & incident notification process

Alan — thanks for the clear list. I've attached a short TAM Coverage Summary and our Incident Comm Playbook that address each point. High level answers here so your team can move quickly: 

- TAM coverage: standard coverage is 9am–6pm local business days (Mon–Fri) for Hosted customers. Dedicated customers can opt in to 24/7 on-call for an additional monthly fee; we staff 3 overlap rotations for Dedicated 24/7 coverage.
- SLAs (initial response): Sev P1: 15 minutes (Dedicated with 24/7) / 1 hour (Hosted business-hours); Sev P2: 1 hour (Dedicated) / 4 hours (Hosted); Sev P3: next-business-day. Full severity definitions in the attached playbook.
- Escalation path: TAM -> Senior TAM -> Support Escalation Engineer -> Product Incident Lead -> Engineering Pager (if RCA required). Contact matrix (phone + email) in the attachment.
- Incident notifications: we push an initial alert within the SLA above, a technical bulletin at 1 hour, status updates every 2 hours for active P1s, and a post-incident RCA within 72 hours (preliminary) and final RCA within 14 days. We also support a bridged conference line for cross-team communications.
- Evidence: SOC2 Type II and audit logs can be shared under NDA; I included our public SOC2 pointer and attached a sanitized copy of the report for procurement review.

Link to live playbook and artifacts: https://drive.redwood/example/Redwood_TAM_Coverage_Summary

If it helps, Marissa (cc'd) will join a call to walk through the matrix later today.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
(Attachment: Redwood_TAM_Coverage_Summary.pdf, Redwood_Incident_Comm_Playbook_v2.pdf)

From: Alan Kerr <alan.kerr@firstnational.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-07-14T11:10:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Service coverage & incident notification process

Ben — appreciate the quick turn. A couple of clarifying items before we bring procurement legal in: 

1) For Dedicated 24/7, is the 15-minute initial response SLA a guarantee (penalty/credit) or a best-effort target?
2) Can you confirm whether the notification cadence for P1s (every 2 hours) can be adjusted to 1 hour for critical financial-impact incidents?
3) Will the bridged conference line include a Redwood moderator, or is it customer-led?

If you can confirm these by EOD we can slot Redwood into our vendor scorecard.

Thanks,
Alan

---
(References: procurement timeline — EOD Jul 15 request)

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alan Kerr <alan.kerr@firstnational.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-07-14T13:30:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Service coverage & incident notification process

Alan — hi, Marissa here (Head of Customer Success). Quick answers and firm commitments for First National: 

1) SLA commitments: For Dedicated 24/7 customers we include the 15-minute initial response as a contractual SLA tied to service credits (described in Section 4 of the attached TAM Coverage Summary). Hosted customers outside business hours are best-effort; we provide escalation guarantees during support-window coverage only.

2) Notification cadence escalation: Yes — for customers with verified financial-impact/revenue-critical incidents we will increase status updates to hourly for P1s upon request. We require that the customer designate the incident as "financial criticality" in the incident intake form so our team can apply the tighter cadence and route to an incident commander.

3) Bridge moderation: Redwood provides a moderator/incident coordinator for P1s at Dedicated-tier customers when on-call is enabled. For Hosted accounts, Redwood can provide a bridge on request during major incidents but dedicated moderation is part of the Dedicated 24/7 package.

Attached is a one-page coverage matrix that maps coverage tiers to phone/contact points and expected response times. Also included is a sanitized SOC2 Type II evidence packet we can share under our standard NDA.

Next steps I propose: 1) confirm Dedicated 24/7 opt-in on the procurement term sheet; 2) we will supply purchase order language for SLA credits; 3) schedule a 30m walkthrough with your ops team tomorrow morning (Jul 15 09:00 PT suggestion).

Regards,
Marissa Cole
Head of Customer Success, Redwood Inference
(Attachment: Redwood_TAM_Coverage_Summary.pdf, SOC2-Type-II-Report-2026.pdf)

---
On Thu Jul 14 10:22, Ben Carter wrote:
> Alan — thanks for the clear list...

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alan Kerr <alan.kerr@firstnational.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-07-15T08:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Service coverage & incident notification process

Alan — Karthik on the commercial team. If you confirm the Dedicated 24/7 opting and the standard 15-minute P1 SLA in the term sheet, I can add the associated discount for a multi-year commit and include the SLA credit language in the appendix.

Can you confirm whether First National requires local phone numbers for escalation (US-only is fine) or global numbers by region? This affects our onboarding timeline.

Thanks,
Karthik Iyer
Senior Enterprise Account Executive, Redwood Inference

(Quick note: added to thread to help finalize procurement language.)

From: Alan Kerr <alan.kerr@firstnational.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-07-15T15:40:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Service coverage & incident notification process

Team — thanks. Confirming a path forward: 

- First National will include Dedicated 24/7 as an option on the shortlist; we want the 15-minute P1 SLA to be contractual (with credits) for Dedicated.
- We will request hourly status cadence for P1s that are labeled as "financial criticality"; procurement/legal will add the incident definition clause.
- Local US phone escalation points are sufficient for our initial rollout.

Marissa — please share the onboarding timeline and the PO language Karthik referenced. Also please forward the sanitized SOC2 copy to procurement@firstnational.com — we have a short secure-doc intake for vendor artifacts.

I'll plan to review the two attached PDFs and will reach out if legal needs follow-up. Appreciate the quick turnaround on this.

Alan Kerr
Sourcing Manager, First National Bank

title: Runtime access carveout & subprocessor acceptance path
preview:
From: Lena Ortiz <lena@heliohealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: contracts@heliohealth.com
Date: Mon, 12 Jul 2027 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: DPA redlines: runtime-access carveout + list of subprocessors

Hi Ben —

Thanks again for the demo last week. Our legal team attached a marked-up DPA (see helio_dpa_redlines_v3.pdf) with two items they consider blockers for our security review:

1) An explicit runtime-access carveout that would allow Helio to request transient runtime KV cache dumps for debugging in our environment (we'd like to limit this to a small set of sessions and require on-demand approvals).
2) A requirement to approve a minimal list of subprocessors that will handle inference runtime telemetry; the wording in Section 7.2 is currently too broad for our procurement.

Can you route this to your product/security leads and confirm what acceptance path you have for these two items? We need an answer before our procurement window next Friday.

Thanks,
Lena Ortiz
Head of Integrations
Helio Health

Attachments: helio_dpa_redlines_v3.pdf

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Lena Ortiz <lena@heliohealth.com>
Cc: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Jul 2027 10:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: DPA redlines: runtime-access carveout + list of subprocessors

Lena — thanks for sending the marked-up DPA and flagging the timing. Looping in Olga (Security) and Karthik (Product) to confirm feasibility and the approval path.

Summary of asks I took from your note:
- Limited, auditable runtime-access carveout for Helio (on-demand, session-scoped).
- Approval flow for a minimal subprocessor list tied to runtime telemetry (Helio wants to approve named vendors).

Planned next steps:
- Olga: can you confirm whether Security can support a controlled runtime dump process and the audit controls we would need?
- Karthik: from a product/runtime standpoint, is there an ops-safe way to produce session-level artifacts without broadening our support surface?
- I'll prepare a short exec note if we need an exception to the existing runtime telemetry policy.

Ben
Sr. Manager, Sales Engineering
Redwood Inference

On Mon, Jul 12, 2027 at 09:15 AM Lena Ortiz <lena@heliohealth.com> wrote:
> [original message attached]


From: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Lena Ortiz <lena@heliohealth.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Jul 2027 15:09:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: DPA redlines: runtime-access carveout + list of subprocessors

Ben / team — I reviewed the marked-up DPA (thanks Lena). Quick notes from a security & compliance lens:

- Runtime-access carveout: We can support a narrow, auditable process, but we need the following controls explicitly spelled out in the DPA or an attached SOP:
  * Authorization model: who at Helio can request dumps (named users and emergency escalation)
  * Scope: session IDs only, TTL for artifacts (48 hours max), and a keystore-binding so dumps are encrypted at rest
  * Approval cadence: a one-time review + per-request justification logged; all requests must be approved by Redwood Security on a case-by-case basis unless executive exception is signed.

- Subprocessor list: We do not currently publish an exhaustive runtime telemetry vendor list (it changes with infra). Security can provide a minimal "runtime-telemetry" group (currently: Telemetrix Inc., ByteTrace Labs) that we can expose for approval. However, we cannot commit to a static list that blocks environment-level upgrades — instead propose an approver notification model: Helio approves the initial list; Redwood must notify Helio of additions 30 days before activation with a right to pause for procurement discussion.

If legal wants exact contract language, we can draft required clauses (SOP + runtime-carveout appendix) but we'll need product to confirm that a 48-hour TTL and per-request approval are operationally acceptable.

Olga
Head of Security & Compliance
Redwood Inference

On Mon, Jul 12, 2027 at 10:02 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> [summary and routing to security/product]


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Lena Ortiz <lena@heliohealth.com>
Date: Tue, 13 Jul 2027 09:21:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: DPA redlines: runtime-access carveout + list of subprocessors

Thanks — product perspective below. Two important constraints to call out:

1) Session-level dumps are feasible, but producing them on-demand requires a support path that touches live KV caches and checkpointing. To keep this safe we need:
   - A maintenance window for any dump that could impact latency-sensitive customers (we can make it near-zero for single-session cases, but ops will throttle if concurrent).
   - A small engineering task (~4-6 days) to build a hardened export runbook and automation that applies the encryption and TTL Olga mentioned. We can turn this into a short SOP attachment to the DPA.

2) The "30-day notification + right to pause" model for subprocessor additions is acceptable, but product requests an explicit carveout for emergency infra changes (zero-day fixes) where we will notify as soon as possible after activation and provide an emergency brief. This mirrors our existing infra change process.

I'd propose the following path:
- Product will scope and deliver the automation & SOP (ETA: 2027-07-22).
- Security provides the draft contract language and the initial approved vendor list by 2027-07-16.
- Ben / Sales to get Helio to agree to the SOP + 48-hour TTL + notification model; if Helio needs a different TTL we escalate for exec approval.

Karthik
Group PM, Runtime
Redwood Inference

> On Mon, Jul 12, 2027 at 03:09 PM Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.com> wrote:
> > [security notes]


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Lena Ortiz <lena@heliohealth.com>, Tom Brooks <tbrooks@heliohealth.com>
Cc: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 14 Jul 2027 15:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: DPA redlines: runtime-access carveout + list of subprocessors

Lena / Tom —

Thanks for your patience. Quick update and proposed acceptance path so procurement can proceed:

1) Security will provide contract language for a Runtime-Carveout Appendix and an "On-demand Dump SOP". We will attach SOP to the DPA and require per-request approval + 48-hour TTL (Olga to deliver by 2027-07-16).
2) Product will deliver the automation and the export runbook (Karthik ETA 2027-07-22). This will include encryption-at-rest and a one-click approval workflow captured in the SOP.
3) Subprocessor model: Redwood will provide an initial minimal list (Telemetrix Inc., ByteTrace Labs). Redwood will notify Helio of additions 30 days prior; exception for emergency infra fixes (post-activation notification).

Action items for Helio: please confirm acceptance of the above three bullets by end-of-day Friday (2027-07-16) so we can proceed with SOP attachments and a formal DPA update. If Helio needs a shorter TTL or a different approver model please indicate which item so we can escalate.

I've attached an exec-approval-note.docx in case Legal/Procurement need an internal sign-off from us while we formalize the DPA language. If you prefer, we can set a 30-minute call Thursday to walk through the SOP and vendor list.

Best,
Ben Carter
Sr. Manager, Sales Engineering
Redwood Inference

Attachments: exec-approval-note.docx, runtime-access-matrix.xlsx

On Tue, Jul 13, 2027 at 09:21 AM Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com> wrote:
> [product ETA and constraints]

title: Pilot uplift and staged residency activation credit discussion
preview:
From: Alexandra Greene <alexandra.greene@medbridgehealth.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@medbridgehealth.com
Date: Mon, 12 Jul 2027 15:03:00 -0700
Subject: Pilot uplift ask — credits for residency activation + HSM evidence

Hi Kimberly / Ben,

Thanks again for the demo last week. We loved the latency profile but have a few open questions on economics before procurement routes this to legal. Two asks we want you to consider for the pilot -> production path: 

1) Pilot uplift credit: can Redwood provide a one-time credit to cover the incremental cost of enabling HSM seats and auditlog ingest during our 90-day pilot? That avoids a large one-off implementation invoice on our side.
2) Staged residency activation: we plan a phased go-live (pilot in us-west, prod in us-east). Can the residency activation fee be amortized as a monthly credit against our committed throughput for the first 12 months instead of a single upfront charge?

High level constraints from procurement: we need clear amortization math (monthly credit schedule), an evidence-delivery milestone we can use to gate credits, and an approach that keeps our capital approvals simple. Would you be able to sketch terms (numbers or ranges) so procurement can model the P&L?

Thanks — happy to do a 20-minute call if easier.

Alexandra
Procurement Manager — MedBridge Health
procurement@medbridgehealth.com
(415) 555-0192

Attachment: requirements-checklist.xlsx (spreadsheet stub)

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>
To: Alexandra Greene <alexandra.greene@medbridgehealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 13 Jul 2027 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pilot uplift ask — credits for residency activation + HSM evidence

Alexandra —

Appreciate the clarity on constraints. Summary of our proposed construct below; I converted the asks into actionable bullets so procurement can plug numbers into modeling. Ben, tagging you for the pricing rows and to attach the credit matrix. Laura, heads-up that this would be a concession framework if approved.

Proposal (high level):
- Pilot uplift credit: one-time credit equal to the incremental OPEX for HSM seats + auditlog K ingestion for the 90-day pilot. We recommend mapping this to a capped credit (example $XXk) rather than line-item pass-through.
- Residency activation: convert the one-time residency activation fee into a 12-month amortized credit against committed monthly throughput. Example: $120k activation -> $10k/mo credit for months 1–12, applied as a bill credit against tokens consumed.
- Evidence & milestone gating: credits tied to two deliverables — (a) HSM attestation & key rotation plan (week 4), (b) evidence package for auditlog ingest (end of pilot). Failure to deliver reduces credit by a defined percent (we can draft exact language).
- SOW implication: we will include an exhibit that enumerates the credit schedule and the evidence acceptance criteria.

Next steps: Ben to provide a nominal credit matrix (range-based) and SOW draft. Alexandra, if you can send any procurement templates or preferred milestone language that helps us standardize acceptance criteria, that would speed the legal loop.

Best,
Kimberly
Kimberly Park
Director, Enterprise Sales — Redwood Inference
kimberly_park@redwood.com
Attachment: none

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Alexandra Greene <alexandra.greene@medbridgehealth.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 14 Jul 2027 08:15:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pilot uplift ask — credits for residency activation + HSM evidence

Alexandra —

Per Kim's note, attached is a draft credit matrix and a high-level SOW excerpt that shows how credits flow and the acceptance gates. Numbers below are illustrative; we can firm up once procurement confirms target credit amount or % of pilot spend.

Quick numbers (illustrative):
- Pilot uplift: capped credit = $18,500 (covers 2 HSM seats @ pilot equivalent + auditlog ingestion estimate).
- Residency activation: $96,000 activation fee -> amortized as $8,000/mo credit for first 12 months against committed throughput.
- Evidence acceptance: credits paid back if evidence not provided within 45 days after the milestone (linear clawback at 50% of the credit).

I've attached: redwood-compliance-credit-matrix.xlsx (shows rows for pilot, residency, HSM seats, auditlog retention) and SOW-draft-MedBridge.pdf (exhibit language for milestone gating).

If those ranges look reasonable, we can mark this a limited concession and add it to the draft commercial summary for your procurement review. Want me to convert these into a 3-line summary for legal?

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive — Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.com
Attachment: redwood-compliance-credit-matrix.xlsx (spreadsheet stub), SOW-draft-MedBridge.pdf (doc stub)

Please proceed to: (A) convert the credit matrix into a 1-page commercial term sheet, (B) add explicit acceptance checklist items into the SOW exhibit, and (C) include a clause that credits roll off if the customer reduces committed throughput below the pilot anchor after month 6.

From: Elisa Ramos <elisa.ramos@novopulse.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: finance@novopulse.com
Date: Fri, 10 Jul 2027 09:02:00 -0700
Subject: Billing split request for Alpha Group (UK + DE entities)

Hi Ava,

Following our renewal call, Finance at Alpha Group has asked us to issue separate invoice obligations for the two legal entities that will be remitting payment: Alpha Ltd (UK) and Alpha GmbH (DE). Summary of the split they want: 70% payable by Alpha Ltd (UK) and 30% payable by Alpha GmbH (DE).

Requirements from their AP team:
- Two invoice numbers (one per legal entity) reflecting their respective VAT treatment.
- Visible VAT registration for each invoice (we've provided both VAT IDs below).
- Each invoice should include a cost center tag so internal GL mapping at Alpha works on receipt.
- They want a single remittance advice PDF for their bank to process but the invoices must be separate.

VAT / legal names / VAT IDs (source: Alpha AP):
- Alpha Ltd (UK) - Company name: Alpha Limited, VAT: GB123456789
- Alpha GmbH (DE) - Company name: Alpha GmbH, VAT: DE987654321

Can Redwood issue two invoices with the above split and confirm the cost center field mapping we can populate on the invoice PDF? Also attaching a quick suggested payment schedule in case it helps (we're open to doing net30 combined submission but separate payments to each entity).

Attachment: payment_schedule_proposal.xlsx (stub)

Thanks,
Elisa Ramos
Head of Finance Operations
NovoPulse

To: Elisa Ramos <elisa.ramos@novopulse.com>

Date: Fri, 10 Jul 2027 11:20:00 -0700

Hi Elisa — thanks for the clear breakdown. Yes, we can do this; outlining next steps so accounting and AP have a single source of truth: 

1) Redwood billing will generate two invoice PDFs with separate invoice numbers (one per legal entity). Each PDF will include: legal name, VAT ID, and a custom field labeled \"Alpha_cost_center\" where we will write exactly the cost-center string you provide.

2) We'll apply a 70/30 split to the invoice amounts (we'll round to the nearest cent and include a short line item noting the split for auditability).

3) We can attach a single remittance advice PDF for your bank; our billing ops will include it in the delivery bundle and set the file name to \"remittance_advice.pdf\".

Questions for you (please confirm by EOD):
- Exact Alpha cost center strings for UK and DE (format e.g. CC-12345-ENG).
- Preferred invoice date and desired payment terms (net30 / net45).

title: Datadog log retention addendum — options, pricing, and SOW
preview:
From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.com>
To: renewals@datadog.com, Evan Liu <evan.liu@datadog.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Jul 2027 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Datadog log retention addendum — options, pricing, and SOW

Hi Evan / Datadog renewals team,

We have our Datadog contract up for amendment focused on log retention and indexing (current term ends 2028-01-31). Usage has spiked and the renewal quote we received lists a ~38% uplift due to added ingestion and retention add-ons. Before we escalate to execs, can you provide: 

- A compact comparison of three alternatives: (1) 30-day hot retention with archive-to-s3 for 1 year, (2) 60-day hot retention with selective indexing, (3) current 90-day hot retention but with a committed usage discount. Include per-GB and monthly costs for each (with and without standard 12-mo commit).
- Any migration mechanics or SOW items we should expect (e.g., agent config changes, reindexing windows).
- If there are feature tradeoffs (saved queries, alerts, metric derivation) with selective indexing.

Cc'ing Rafael (sales ops), Laura (finance) and Dylan (infra) to keep stakeholders aligned. We're particularly sensitive to: reducing monthly unit cost while retaining ability to run long-tail log searches for incident debug and retaining audit logs for 1 year for compliance.

I've attached the renewal quote Datadog sent and our draft SOW template for retention work so you can mark where changes would land. Goal: get a decision path this week so procurement can issue PO and we can sign the amendment before EoQ.

Thanks,
Camila

--
Camila Reyes
Sr. Procurement Manager | Redwood Inference
camila.reyes@redwood.com
(415) 555-0192

Attachments: Datadog_renewal_quote_July2027.pdf, SOW-logging-retention-amendment.docx


From: Evan Liu <evan.liu@datadog.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.com>
Cc: renewals@datadog.com
Date: Mon, 12 Jul 2027 11:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Datadog log retention addendum — options, pricing, and SOW

Hi Camila — thanks for the clear ask and the SOW. Quick summary below; attaching an annotated quote and a short SOW redline.

Option A — 30-day hot + 11-mo archive-to-S3:
- Hot retention: 30 days for indexed logs; archival storage in S3 for up to 12 months (restore via on-demand retrieval).
- Cost: $0.018/GB hot + $0.0025/GB/month archive (example blended monthly for your current 25TB/month: ~$6.75k hot + ~$1.56k archive = ~$8.3k/mo).
- Tradeoffs: longer restore latency for older logs; saved views remain but archived logs not searchable until restored.

Option B — 60-day hot with selective indexing:
- Hot retention: 60 days for fully indexed logs, plus selective indexing for noisy sources (sampled or non-indexed raw stored cheaper).
- Cost: $0.028/GB hot if fully indexed; selective indexing reduces effective bill by ~25-40% depending on sources. For your telemetry mix, we estimate ~$11k/mo without commit; with a 12-mo 15% commit discount estimate ~$9.35k/mo.

Option C — 90-day hot with committed capacity:
- Keep current experience; 12-mo commit pricing could bring the uplift down to ~+10% YoY vs quoted non-committed price.
- Cost: ~13% higher than your current invoice without commit; with commit approx +6% relative to current.

Other notes:
- One-time migration SOW: ~2.5 engineer-days for agent config and pipeline updates plus up to 8 hours for QA and runbook updates. Redline attached.
- We can provide a complimentary 30-day reduce-invoice credit if you sign the 12-mo commit this quarter (must be on docuSign by 31-Jul-2027).

I've attached: annotated_quote.pdf and SOW_redline.docx. Happy to hop on a 30-min call with Rafael/Dylan/Laura to walk through numbers.

Best,
Evan
Senior Renewals Manager — Datadog
evan.liu@datadog.com

Attachments: annotated_quote.pdf, SOW_redline.docx


From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.com>
To: Evan Liu <evan.liu@datadog.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Jul 2027 13:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Datadog log retention addendum — options, pricing, and SOW

Thanks Evan — helpful level of detail. Internal quick reactions below (reply-all to keep alignment):

- Rafael: Option B looks promising from a product/eng perspective (keeps searchability for 60 days). Can you quantify which sources to selectively index? I think we can sample noisy infra logs (k8s kubelet, node-exporter) but keep app logs fully indexed.
- Laura: the 12-mo commit + 15% discount + your 30-day signing credit could be the least friction to procurement if the overall ARR delta is acceptable. Can you confirm if the commit requires an upfront payment or monthly amortization?
- Dylan: migration SOW of 2.5 eng-days looks reasonable; we may prefer to own the agent changes but want Datadog to support rollout verification and QA.

Action items: Evan, please confirm commit billing cadence and share the annotated quote as a named PDF (we referenced a generic PDF but need the formal quote for PO). Rafael, Laura and Dylan — if you can OK the direction today I’ll ask procurement to prepare the PO and route for signature. We should also keep the SOW with minor redlines and send to legal for a quick review.

Camila

> 
> [snip quoted options A/B/C]
> 
> Best,
> Evan

Subject: Re: Datadog log retention addendum — options, pricing, and SOW

Decision: move forward with Option B (60-day hot + selective indexing) with a 12-month commit at the 15% discount, subject to confirmation from Evan on billing cadence (monthly amortization preferred).

Budget/PO: Laura — request a PO for $112,200 ARR (this reflects the committed price in Evan's estimate). Please route to AP once the DocuSign agreement is received.

SOW & implementation: Dylan, you'll own the agent config changes; Evan to provide the SOW redline and a 2-hr onboarding session. We keep 90-day archive for audit logs in S3 (in case legal requests).

Hi team —

Thanks for the interest in Redwood. I’m Aisha (AE) and I’ll coordinate the kickoff and mutual action plan between our teams. Suggested attendees on the Redwood side: me (AE), Karthik (SE) and Monica (CS). From your side, we expect procurement + an engineering/security representative.

To get the procurement process moving, can you confirm a preferred 60-min kickoff in one of these windows (all PDT):
- Tue Jul 13, 10:00–11:00
- Wed Jul 14, 13:00–14:00
- Thu Jul 15, 09:00–10:00

title: Pricing and burst-credit structure for parallel team onboarding / new workload
preview:
From: Sarah Kim <sarah@nimbushealth.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 11 Jul 2027 08:03:00 -0700
Subject: Question on per-token burst credits + commit tiers for new product team

Hi Camila,

We're kicking off a new ML-driven feature (patient-sentiment) that will be a separate product stream and might run in parallel with two existing teams. We expect a high initial burst in tokens during training/eval cycles and then a steady lower throughput.

Can you share:

title: rw-storm-6b: elevated HTTP errors & short-term mitigation
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 6 Jul 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: rw-storm-6b: elevated HTTP errors & short-term mitigation

Monica / Aditya,

We are seeing an uptick in 5xx and timeout responses for rw-storm-6b starting ~08:50 PDT. Error spike appears concentrated on longer-context generation jobs (>= 1024 tokens) and on a handful of BrightBank customers routing to the model. Initial signs point to memory backpressure on a subset of GPU hosts when KV cache grows quickly.

What I've done so far:
- Scaled up instance pool for rw-storm-6b by +20% (warm nodes coming up).
- Flipped aggressive prefix cache eviction to free KV memory on the busiest hosts.
- Notified BrightBank ops; holding a sync at 10:00 to share temporary mitigations.

Immediate ask:
- Aditya: can you validate kernel OOM/restart logs on host pool gw-rw-6b-*/ and share any related metrics?
- Monica: prepare a short customer-facing blurb (2-3 sentences) to post to status and send to BrightBank. Keep it factual and avoid root cause until we confirm.

I'll follow up with an update before the 10:00 call.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.ai
Redwood Inference



From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: BrightBank Ops <ops@brightbank.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 6 Jul 2027 09:28:15 -0700
Subject: rw-storm-6b: brief notice — elevated errors impacting some requests

Hi Sarah / BrightBank team,

We wanted to let you know we are investigating an increase in 5xx and timeout errors affecting certain rw-storm-6b requests, primarily long-context generations. We have deployed interim capacity and cache-eviction changes to reduce impact and are tracking the situation closely.

Immediate impact: intermittent failed/gateway errors for generation calls; customers may see retries or higher latency.
Planned next steps: engineering is collecting host-level logs and will push a mitigation patch if necessary.

We will provide a status update at 10:00 PDT with mitigation progress. If you have sample requests that reproduced the error, please reply with a redacted example and timestamps so we can correlate.

Apologies for the interruption — we appreciate your patience.

Regards,
Ben Carter

From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 7 Jul 2027 11:05:31 -07:00
Subject: Re: rw-storm-6b: elevated HTTP errors & short-term mitigation

Ben / Monica,

Root-cause signal: several hosts serving rw-storm-6b experienced sustained KV cache growth when clients sent long-prefix prompts and kept KV pinned (no forced eviction). Under heavy concurrency, memory pressure triggered a host-side worker restart causing 502/504s. Evidence: repeated GPU memory fragmentation warnings and the kernel OOM logs I attached to the ticket.

Mitigations applied:
- Rebalanced traffic away from three noisy hosts (gw-rw-6b-13/14/19) and started replacement warm nodes.
- Enabled more aggressive per-session KV eviction for requests > 1500 tokens to prevent unbounded growth.
- Added a temporary routing rule to prefer rw-temp-6b-compat as a fallback for sessions with ctx > 1200 tokens.

Next steps:
1) Patch runtime to proactively trim KV after response send (INF-3421) — ETA 24h to rollout to staging, 48h to prod if tests pass.
2) Run a job to surface long-lived pinned sessions and notify customers with recommendations.
3) Provide BrightBank recommended fallback config (model and new retry settings).

I've uploaded host logs to the Jira ticket and attached rw-storm-6b-prefix-logs-0707.tar.gz.

- Aditya
Principal Infra Engineer, Redwood Inference
aditya@redwood.ai



From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: BrightBank Ops <ops@brightbank.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 8 Jul 2027 16:45:00 -07:00
Subject: rw-storm-6b: recovery update and recommended short-term fallback

Hi Sarah / BrightBank team,

Quick update following our mitigation work:

Summary:
- Impact window: Jul 6 08:50 PDT — Jul 8 15:40 PDT (gradual recovery as replacement nodes fully warmed).
- Root cause: uncontrolled KV cache growth on rw-storm-6b under long-context concurrent workloads leading to host restarts and 5xx/timeout errors.
- Status: We have rebalanced traffic, enabled KV eviction, and provisioned additional warm hosts. Production error rate for rw-storm-6b is now back to baseline (<0.2% 5xx).

What we recommend for BrightBank (short-term):
- Add a model fallback to rw-temp-6b-compat for requests where ctx_len > 1200. This reduces memory pressure and keeps latency predictable.
- Reduce client-side retry window to 6s and add exponential backoff (base 300ms) to avoid compounding load.

Artifacts and next steps:
- Logs and analysis: https://redwood.atlassian.net/browse/INF-3421 (contains attached logs and timeline).
- ETA for runtime patch to proactively trim KV: 48h for prod rollout (we will keep you posted).
- We'll schedule a follow-up sync next week to review any changes to your integration and plan the fallback deployment.

Attachments: rw-storm-6b-prefix-logs-0707.tar.gz (host logs), customer-error-sample-20270707.txt

If you'd like us to apply the fallback on your behalf for a short window, we can coordinate a low-risk cutover — let me know preferred time windows.

Thanks again for flagging this, and sorry for the disruption.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.ai
Redwood Inference

title: Apex: platform degradation — executive liaison and customer update
preview:
From: Emily Park <emily.park@apexretail.com>
To: support@redwood.ai, ben.carter@redwood.ai
Date: Wed, 7 Jul 2027 08:55:00 -0700
Subject: High latency + 5xx spike on /v1/generate — 8:40am PDT

Hi team,

We started seeing a spike in latency and ~3% 5xx errors at ~08:40 PDT across our busiest routes (chat + generation). Customer-facing pages are timing out intermittently and the cart flow degraded for a small subset of users. Can you confirm you see this on your side? We need an ETA and any possible mitigation (route change / throttling) we should apply.

I've attached a short HAR plus some aggregated telemetry from our side (apex-summary-0800.json).

Thanks,
Emily

Technical Program Manager | Apex Retail

Phone: +1 415-555-0133

To: Emily Park <emily.park@apexretail.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Grace Oconnor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 7 Jul 2027 09:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: High latency + 5xx spike on /v1/generate — 8:40am PDT

Emily — thanks for the signal, and sorry for the disruption. I’m standing up an exec liaison path and an internal command thread.

Immediate actions:
- Marissa: lead customer comms cadence and keep Emily updated every 30 min.
- Grace + Aditya: analyze telemetry, confirm whether this is routing, model load, or infra.
- I’ll engage our capacity team and run a targeted fallback to the last known-good model variant if we confirm model-correlated degradation.

Emily: can you paste a few sample request IDs (x-request-id) from 08:35–08:50 so we can correlate logs? Also, we got the HAR, thanks.

Ben

Director, Customer Reliability
Redwood Inference



From: Grace Oconnor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 7 Jul 2027 09:18:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: High latency + 5xx spike on /v1/generate — telemetry snapshot

Quick telemetry snapshot:
- At 08:38 UTC-7 we see a sharp rise in P95 token latency from 380ms -> 1.8s on the inference cluster serving ap-south-west-1 model pool A.
- 5xxs spiked to ~3.1% concentrated on the /v1/generate route hitting the long-seq path.
- KV cache eviction rate doubled; we saw a correlated increase in memory pressure on nodes with model-quant-8 profiles.
- No obvious network egress failures; load balancer healthy.

Attaching a trimmed telemetry bundle (apex-telemetry-0707.zip). Investigating whether a recent kernel selection adjustment (deployed 07/07 02:00 UTC) caused regressions for long-seq.

Grace
Lead SRE, Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference

Attachments: apex-telemetry-0707.zip (attachment stub)


From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
To: Grace Oconnor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 7 Jul 2027 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: High latency + 5xx spike on /v1/generate — mitigation proposals

Heads-up on workable mitigations while we root cause:
1) Roll back the kernel-selection tweak (deployed 02:00 UTC) for the affected pool — low risk, 6–10 min to complete and validate.
2) Temporarily route long-seq traffic to a dedicated fallback model-variant (quant-4) with slightly higher cost but more stable memory profile — ~5% quality delta but immediate latency improvement.
3) Increase per-tenant concurrency cap for Apex for 30 minutes (if they approve) to reduce queuing/P95.

I recommend #1 + #2 in parallel; I can execute rollback and stage the fallback. Need Ben/Marissa to approve sending a short customer-facing message that mentions a targeted fallback and ETA ~20–30 min.

Aditya
Principal Infra Engineer



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Emily Park <emily.park@apexretail.com>, procurement@apexretail.com
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Grace Oconnor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 7 Jul 2027 10:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: High latency + 5xx spike on /v1/generate — immediate mitigation and ETA

Emily / Apex team — update and immediate plan: 

What we see: a regression affecting long-sequence generation introduced after a runtime kernel selector change earlier today. This increased memory pressure and caused higher eviction/5xx behavior on a small subset of nodes serving your traffic.

What we’re doing (in progress):
- Rolling back the kernel-selection change on the affected pool (started 10:10 PDT) — expected 8–12 minutes to complete and validate.
- Staging a traffic-safe fallback to our quant-4 model variant for long-seq requests; will route only Apex traffic and fall back automatically to the prior variant after verification.
- Increasing short-term concurrency cap for your tenant by 15% for the next 30 minutes to reduce queuing.

Expected customer impact: we expect P95 to return to prior baseline within 20–35 minutes. A small subset of long-seq calls may see a slight quality delta while fallback is in effect.

Attachments: sla-exception-proposal-v1.pdf (internal proposed wording for exec approval)

Marissa will post progress updates every 30 minutes; earliest full update 10:45 PDT. Please send any specific request IDs if you want deeper correlations.

Ben Carter
Director, Customer Reliability
Redwood Inference

To: Emily Park <emily.park@apexretail.com>

Date: Wed, 7 Jul 2027 10:42:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: High latency + 5xx spike on /v1/generate — customer update 10:42 PDT

Emily — quick status for your customers and stakeholders: 
- Rollback completed at 10:30 PDT; early validation shows P95 trending back toward baseline (P95 now ~420ms).
- Fallback variant is in flight for long-seq traffic; seeing immediate reduction in 5xx to ~0.6%.
- We’re continuing to monitor for 60 minutes and will revert fallback after validating quality and stability.

Next update scheduled 11:15 PDT. If you’d like a concise one-pager for your executives, we can produce a short incident summary with timeline and mitigation notes (attachable).

Thanks for bearing with us — we’ll keep you posted.

Marissa Cole
Senior Customer Success Manager
Redwood Inference

title: Short-window concurrency top-up & credit split for launch
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Sonia Patel <sonia@launchly.com>
Date: Thu, 07 Jul 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Short-window concurrency top-up & credit split for launch

Sonia —

Congrats on the upcoming launch next week. We reviewed your forecast and can provide an emergency concurrency top-up to cover the expected burst for launch-day traffic. High-level proposal: 

- Scope: Temporary concurrency increase to 2.5x baseline capacity for a 72-hour window (UTC: 2027-07-13 00:00 — 2027-07-16 00:00).
- Billing: Overrun consumption during the window would be billed at standard PAYG rates. As a one-time concession we can apply a credit equal to 35% of the billed overrun (capped at $15,000) applied on the next invoice.
- Requirements: Signed addendum + PO by EOD Friday (2027-07-09) to ensure provisioning and a reserved headroom pool.
- Attachments: draft addendum stub included if you want to review language.

If procurement needs a different framing (e.g., prepay vs post-invoice credit) let me know — we can present two options. Quick asks: can you confirm the launch start/end in UTC and whether you want extra throughput in APAC?

Thanks —
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0123

Attachment: launchly_burst_addendum_v1.pdf (application/pdf, 42KB)


From: Sonia Patel <sonia@launchly.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 07 Jul 2027 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Short-window concurrency top-up & credit split for launch

Ben —

Appreciate the quick turnaround and flexibility. A few follow-ups from procurement & engineering: 

1) The 72-hour window is correct (starts 2027-07-13 00:00 UTC).
2) Procurement prefers a documented addendum with exact billing language; their team will only accept credits tied to specific invoice line-items.
3) The credit cap is OK, but they want a larger share — can Redwood offer a split where Launchly receives a 50% credit on the first 24 hours of overrun and 25% on subsequent hours? They argue launch-day risk is concentrated in the first day.
4) Also need clarity on how rollback happens if we spike beyond 3x — are requests throttled or will you auto-failover to a cheaper variant?

If we can get the 50/25 split for the 72h window and a simple rollback/fallback description in the addendum, Procurement will push the PO through next week.

Sonia Patel

sonia@launchly.com

To: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 07 Jul 2027 13:25:00 -0700
Subject: FW: Launchly request — concession ask (50/25 split)

Karthik / Marissa —

Quick context: Launchly asked for a 50% credit on first 24h and 25% thereafter for a 72h emergency top-up. They need signed addendum + PO by next week. Engineering will be fine with 2.5x; they also want explicit rollback behavior documented.

Options I see: 
A) Approve 50%/25% split as Sonia requested, cap credit at $20k (higher than initial ask), finance-signed. Fastest commercially but increases concession.
B) Counter with my original 35% flat credit capped at $15k (favors Redwood cash).
C) Hybrid: 45% first 24h, 25% thereafter, cap $17.5k — aligns both sides.

Notes: legal will need a short addendum, procurement wants invoice-line credit language. This is a mid-market ARR deal (expected $280k ARR) — concession might be reasonable to lock the expansion. If you approve a hybrid (C), I can send the addendum and push for PO.

Ben

> Appreciate the quick turnaround and flexibility. A few follow-ups from procurement & engineering: ...

Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 07 Jul 2027 17:03:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Short-window concurrency top-up & credit split for launch

Sonia / Procurement team —

Thanks for the specificity. Redwood can agree the following commercial terms for the temporary top-up: 

- Window: 2027-07-13 00:00 UTC — 2027-07-16 00:00 UTC (72 hours).
- Provisioning: Reserved headroom to 2.5x baseline capacity for the window; will be provisioned after signed addendum + PO.
- Billing & credit: Overrun billed at standard PAYG rates. Redwood will apply a one-time invoice credit equal to 45% of billed overrun during the first 24 hours of the window and 25% of billed overrun for hours 25-72. Total credit cap = $17,500. Credit will be applied to the invoice following the event and will be shown as a separate line item.
- Fallback behavior: If consumption exceeds the reserved pool by >20% we will: 1) throttle to the safety floor for non-critical routes; 2) initiate failover to pre-agreed cheaper model variants for non-essential traffic. Engineering will work with your SREs to pre-tag critical routes.
- Next steps: I attached a short addendum (launchly_burst_addendum_v1.pdf) and our forecast worksheet. Please review and send a PO referencing DL-2027-1189. If procurement prefers prepayment, we can provide an alternative prepay credit structure (less concession).

If these terms look good we’ll route to Legal/Finance and provision the headroom once we have the signed addendum + PO.

Best —
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0123

title: Invoice originator and delivery routing for Brightline AP onboarding
preview:
From: Sara Bradley <sara.bradley@brightlinehealth.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Tom Keller <tkeller@brightlinehealth.com>
Date: Mon, 5 Jul 2027 09:15:00 -0400
Subject: Invoice originator & delivery — Brightline AP onboarding

Hi Ava,

Thanks for the walkthrough last week. We're ready to lock down how Redwood will deliver invoices to Brightline so our AP team can process payments without delays. A couple of questions to confirm requirements before we onboard:

- Do you support sending invoices via a hosted portal (we can pull) or do you e-mail PDFs to our AP address? Which path is the recommended default?
- We have a PO policy: PO must appear on the invoice in 1 of 3 formats (BR-##### or BR-INV-##### or numeric only). Can you confirm where the PO should be placed (invoice header vs line-level)?
- For e-invoicing: do you support XML payloads/SFTP transfer? If so, can you share a sample schema we can validate against?
- Tax/VAT: we operate in multiple states; if we need to supply an EIN or VAT id, what evidence do you require and how is that stored?

I'll attach our typical invoice sample so you can see our PO layout. Please loop in whoever owns billing for Redwood and finance for VAT checks.

Thanks,
Sara Bradley
Director, Revenue Operations
Brightline Health

Attachment: brightline_invoice_sample.pdf

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sara Bradley <sara.bradley@brightlinehealth.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 5 Jul 2027 11:05:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Invoice originator & delivery — Brightline AP onboarding

Hi Sara — great to hear you're ready to proceed. Short answer: we can support both routes, with a few tradeoffs. Summary and recommended next steps below.

1) Delivery options (pros/cons)
- Portal (recommended for recurring, high-volume): we post invoices to a secure Brightline tenant you can pull or we can push via API. Pros: reliable metadata, less PDF parsing, supports XML/e-invoice. Cons: 1–2 business days to onboard tenant and map PO fields.
- Email PDF (fallback or low-volume): we can send a human-readable PDF to ap@brightlinehealth.com and cc any internal contacts. Pros: immediate, low lift. Cons: requires AP to manually match POs unless we also send structured metadata.

2) PO placement and required fields
- Preferred: top-level invoice fields. We'll accept PO in header or line-level if you include a canonical PO tag in the payload. For the portal/XML route, map to field: invoice.po_number. For PDF-only deliveries, include PO in the header and the first line of the PDF.

3) E-invoice options
- We support XML payloads over SFTP, as well as an HTTPS API to push invoices. Attached is our sample e-invoice schema (redwood_einvoice_schema.xml) and a quick portal onboarding checklist (portal_onboarding_instructions.pdf). If you want SFTP instead of portal, that is an option — provide host/key details and we'll coordinate with infra.

4) Tax / VAT evidence
- Looping in Neha (finance ops) for the exact evidence we need. In general we store EIN or VAT ids in our billing profile and attach submitted evidence to the account for audit.

Suggested next steps:
- You confirm preferred delivery: Portal (with email CC to ap@brightlinehealth.com) or SFTP + email copies.
- Brightline confirms PO format to map in our onboarding form (BR-##### vs BR-INV-#####).
- Brightline completes the onboarding form and uploads tax evidence (if applicable).

I'll leave Neha and Rafael to confirm the tax checklist and any contract amendment for e-invoicing if necessary.

Best,
Ava Chen
Account Lead, Revenue
Redwood Inference

Attachments: redwood_einvoice_schema.xml, portal_onboarding_instructions.pdf

From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sara Bradley <sara.bradley@brightlinehealth.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 6 Jul 2027 10:30:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Invoice originator & delivery — Brightline AP onboarding

Hi Sara — quick note from finance on the evidence and VAT handling.

- US customers: we require an EIN for tax recordkeeping if you're requesting any withholding exemptions. Please attach a copy of your W-9 or a scanned EIN letter (or provide the EIN and we will request formal documentation).
- EU customers / cross-border VAT: if you claim reverse charge, provide your VAT registration certificate (pdf). We will store this in our billing profile and reference it on invoices where applicable.

Checklist attached (tax_evidence_checklist.pdf). Once we have the docs we will:
- Mark your billing profile to show the preferred tax treatment (exempt/reverse charge/standard)
- Ensure the invoice has the required tax lines and references for your AP team

Notes on payment collection: our default payment terms for self-serve/portal customers are NET30. For contractually negotiated terms we record payment SLA in the billing profile. Rafael — is there any scenario where Brightline's PO policy requires a contract amendment before we enable e-invoicing?

Thanks,
Neha Kapoor
Manager, Finance Operations
Redwood Inference

Attachment: tax_evidence_checklist.pdf

From: Sara Bradley <sara.bradley@brightlinehealth.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 6 Jul 2027 13:12:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Invoice originator & delivery — Brightline AP onboarding

Thanks Ava and Neha — Brightline prefers the portal as the main delivery channel, but we need a copy of the PDF emailed to ap@brightlinehealth.com for our internal scan/import. SFTP is acceptable if you prefer to push XML there; either way, please make sure the PDF copy contains the PO header in BR-INV-##### format.

We can provide a scanned W-9 (attached below) and will upload any VAT certs if needed. One more ask: can Redwood include a single-line summary field called "billing_reference" that our ERP uses for remittance matching?

Also, for legal: we typically require a one-paragraph amendment to the MSA allowing e-invoice delivery as formal delivery of invoices. If you could loop legal or send a draft amendment, we can sign quickly.

Best,
Sara

> On Mon, Jul 5, 2027 at 11:05 AM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> > Hi Sara — great to hear you're ready to proceed. Short answer: we can support both routes, with a few tradeoffs. Summary and recommended next steps below.

Attachment: brightline_w9_scanned.pdf

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sara Bradley <sara.bradley@brightlinehealth.com>, Accounts Payable <ap@brightlinehealth.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 6 Jul 2027 13:45:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Invoice originator & delivery — Brightline AP onboarding

Thanks — this is helpful and straightforward. Confirming next steps and owners:

1) Delivery path: Portal will be enabled for Brightline tenant. We will also send an emailed PDF copy to ap@brightlinehealth.com on the same cadence as the portal posting. Rafael will coordinate the onboarding schedule with your infra lead and will collect SFTP details if you choose that option.

2) PO mapping: We'll map BR-INV-##### to invoice.po_number and include a top-level "billing_reference" field in the XML and portal metadata to match your ERP. Please confirm the exact sample value format when you complete the onboarding form.

3) Tax evidence: Neha will ingest your W-9 and mark the profile accordingly. If you later provide VAT certificates they will be attached to the profile for EU/cross-border treatments.

4) Legal amendment: Rafael will circulate a one-paragraph amendment to the MSA to explicitly allow e-invoice delivery as formal invoice delivery. Rafael — assigning to you to draft and send via DocuSign.

I'll kick off the portal tenant creation today and share the onboarding link. Rafael — attaching a draft amendment for editing.

Next actions:
- Brightline: finish onboarding form with sample PO value + upload any tax docs (Sara).
- Redwood (Rafael): send amendment-draft-v1.pdf to legal for DocuSign, schedule portal tenant setup, or request SFTP credentials if preferred.
- Redwood (Neha): mark billing profile on receipt of W-9.

Thanks all,
Ava Chen
Account Lead, Revenue
Redwood Inference

Attachments: amendment-draft-v1.pdf
Related link to onboarding: https://redwoodinference.app/onboarding/brightline

title: Dedicated capacity and KV/quantization sizing for BioForge POC
preview:
From: Elena Vargas <elena.vargas@bioforge.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@bioforge.com
Date: 2027-07-01T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity ask — context window and quantization impact

Ben —

Thanks for the demo last week. Procurement asked me to get a concrete proposal for the POC: a dedicated pool to serve our internal clinical summarization model (70B variant) with 64k context. We need an estimate of monthly committed capacity, the GPU type you recommend, and an order-of-magnitude CAPEX/OPEX for our finance team.

Two specific asks from our side:
- KV cache sizing for 64k context and how that changes if we use 32k.
- Guidance on using 4-bit or mixed-precision quantization for the model (memory savings vs. expected latency/regression risk).

Can you send a short annotated plan (numbers + recommended configuration) that we can attach to the procurement packet?

Thanks,
Elena

--
Elena Vargas
Head of ML Infrastructure
BioForge Therapeutics
elena.vargas@bioforge.com


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Vargas <elena.vargas@bioforge.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-07-01T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity ask — context window and quantization impact

Elena —

Appreciate the clear asks. Attaching a short plan and the raw sizing CSV for your finance and infra teams. High level summary below; full numbers are in the attached spreadsheet.

Summary (short):
- Recommended initial dedicated pool: 4x A100-80GB (reserved) with automatic burst to 6x for peak tests.
- Target model: 70B fp16 baseline memory: ~62–68GB for weights on device; with 64k KV cache the working set grows significantly — see CSV.
- With 32k context, KV memory halves (~-50%); recommends starting with 32k for POC to reduce committed footprint, then test 64k in a canary lane.
- Quantization: 4-bit (Q4) recommended for cost-first POC — expected GPU memory reduction ~35–45% on weights, KV caching unchanged. Mixed-precision (fp16 weights + Q4 storage) usually hits the sweet spot for capacity.
- Performance/latency: Q4 can add ~5–12% per-token latency depending on kernel availability; we'll provide compatible kernels in your private deployment.

Next steps I propose:
1) We share the sizing CSV (attached) with your procurement/infra.
2) Redwood will provision a 32k-locked dedicated pool for POC (4x80GB) for 30 days, with an optional 64k canary for two days of stress testing.
3) Joint evaluation on the output quality with your eval set to guard against regressions from quantization.

I'm looping Karthik and Marissa for the detailed memory math and the config we will hand off to your SREs.

Best,
Ben

--
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.com


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-07-02T08:17:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Dedicated capacity ask — context window and quantization impact (technical appendix)

Ben / Marissa —

Technical appendix for Elena (pasted below if you want to send a trimmed version). I also attached kv_cache_sizing.csv (per-layer math) and a short configuration PDF. High level notes:

- Baseline assumptions: 70B model, 1KV per token entry uses 2x (key + value) per layer. For our kernel stack on A100-80GB, per-token KV overhead ~0.6–0.9 MB per 1k tokens across 32 layers (this is a conservative average across Llama-derived 70B variants).

- Example numbers (approx):
  * 32k context: KV ~ 19–28 GB (depends on exact layer dims)
  * 64k context: KV ~ 38–56 GB (rough doubling)
  * Model weights (fp16) on-device: ~62–68 GB
  * Total working set (fp16 + 64k KV): ~100–125 GB — so single 80GB device cannot hold both weights + full KV.

- How we approach this in Redwood Dedicated:
  1) Pool tiling: assign model weights to one GPU and shard KV across the pool with efficient NVLink transfers for local KV hits. This requires a minimum pool of 3 GPUs to keep inter-GPU transfer latency below our SLO for 95th percentile token time.
  2) KV offload: we can offload cold parts of the KV (prefixes not needed immediately) to host RAM with an on-device cache; this reduces committed GPU memory but adds tail latency for cold requests. Recommended for batch-heavy workloads, not latency-critical live agents.
  3) Quantization: converting weights to Q4 reduces weights footprint to ~35–45% of fp16. That can bring the weight component under 30GB, enabling a single 80GB device to hold weights + a reduced KV for shorter contexts (<=32k).

- Suggested configurations for POC (trade-offs):
  Option A — Lower-risk, latency-optimized (start here):
    - 4x A100-80GB (weights sharded + KV distributed).
    - 32k locked context for POC (gives KV headroom).
    - No KV offload initially.
    - Quantization: fp16 production, run Q4 in canary; keeps quality risk minimal.

  Option B — Cost-aware (higher risk):
    - 2x A100-80GB (weights Q4 on single device + KV partial on second), plus host-offload for cold segments.
    - 64k can be supported in this mode but expect higher tail latency and occasional rebuilds under high concurrency.

  Option C — Aggressive cost saves: Q4 everywhere + aggressive KV host-offload — not recommended for low-latency clinical summarization.

- Autoscaling and headroom: for a target concurrency of 20 concurrent sessions (average request ~6k tokens produced + 2k prompt), we estimate 4x80GB reserved with burst to 6x to maintain 95th percentile latency SLO. Exact concurrency math in kv_cache_sizing.csv.

I'll sign off with a short recommended handoff snippet you can share with procurement: 
"Redwood recommends a 30-day reserved pool of 4x A100-80GB, starting with a 32k context cap to validate model quality and throughput. After successful canary, move to a controlled 64k canary week and evaluate Q4 quantization for sustained cost savings."

Karthik

--
Karthik Iyer
Senior Applied ML Engineer | Redwood Inference


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Vargas <elena.vargas@bioforge.com>
Cc: procurement@bioforge.com, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-07-04T16:40:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity and KV/quantization sizing for BioForge POC

Elena —

Looping back with the finalized POC pack (attached):
- Proposal: 30-day reserved 4x A100-80GB pool; 32k context for baseline.
- Optional 48-hour 64k canary for stress testing and functional verification.
- CAPEX/OPEX: see bioforge_capex_estimate.xlsx — rough committed monthly cost line and burst pricing.
- Risk: we'll run Q4 on a canary lane; recommend fp16 as baseline for clinical output fidelity.

If procurement approves, we can sign an MSA addendum and provision the pool within 5 business days. Who should I notify when procurement gives the green light?

Best
Ben

--
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.com

Team —

As discussed in the leadership offsite, attached is an updated alignment memo and a compact org-chart showing the proposed small-team adjustments for Platform and Customer Success. High level:

- Narrower charter for Platform infra (latency/cost initiatives) and a single product lead for inference runtime.
- CS will centralize renewal ownership for top 20 accounts and assign dedicated SEs for Dedicated customers.
- A one-week canary window post-rollout to validate SLAs and pipeline handoffs.

Immediate asks:
- Please review the attached org-chart (org-chart-v2.pdf) and the bullet‑level talking points. I need consolidated feedback and any objections by EOD Monday, Jul 5.
- Laura, can you own town-hall logistics? Marissa, want you to draft the internal FAQ for managers.
- Ben, we should prepare a short note to customers in the top renewal cohort. Can you draft and run by Vivek?

This is intended to be incremental — no headcount moves this quarter, primarily role clarifications and manager-level ownership changes. We need a crisp QBR readout that ties this to OKR progress (Q3 focus: cost/unit, latency P99, renewal NRR for top cohort).

Thanks —
Jonas

Redwood Inference

title: Executive signoff requested: BrightWave pilot latency grace + commercial concession
preview:
From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-07-01T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Request: BrightWave pilot - temporary latency grace and pilot credit

Team,

BrightWave (Samir/Elena copied on thread) just signed up for a 90-day pilot for our inference platform. During the kickoff conversation they asked for two commercial concessions to make the PoC viable for their performance-sensitive use case:

- Temporal latency tolerance: allow 99th percentile tail latency up to 600ms (our standard 99p is 300ms) for the first 90 days of the pilot only.
- Pilot commercial concession: a one-time 20% usage credit for the pilot months, or a cap of $12,000 in total credits (whichever is lower).

Customer rationale: they expect heavy bursty traffic as they run parallel experiments and want to avoid early failures while they tune prompts and caching. They will run the pilot in our us-west region and can accept instrumentation hooks (trace ids, token-level logs) so we can monitor.

Proposed mitigations from AE side: prefix caching enabled, request-level throttling for flagged test runs, and a short-term routing preference to dedicated nodes during peak windows.

Attachments: bw_draft_amendment.pdf (proposed amendment with SLO language), pilot_pricing_sheet.xlsx (discount math).

Asking for:
- Commercial signoff on the 20% pilot credit capped at $12k (Finance approval needed)
- Executive waiver to temporarily allow 99p latency to 600ms for 90 days and to cap service credits at 5% of monthly invoice during that pilot (Legal to confirm non-precedent language).

Reason for exec ask: the customer is on a tight timeline (internal demo 7/15) and wants an explicit amendment they can sign. Without the concession they may pause the pilot.

My recommendation: approve both concessions with written limits: pilot-only clause, explicit auto-revert to standard SLOs after 90 days, monitoring/reporting requirements, and a non-precedent clause.

Please advise — Marissa/Jonas/Sean, can you triage? If we get a greenlight today I can send the amendment for signature and schedule the kickoff.

Thanks,
Avery Johnson
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
avery_johnson@redwood.ai
Mobile: +1 (415) 555-0147

From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>

- Temporal SLO tolerance to 600ms at 99p for exactly 90 days

1) Finance (Sean) must confirm the $12k cap and impact to ARR/recognition.
2) Legal (Jonas) to confirm the amendment language is non-precedential and includes auto-revert and monitoring/reporting obligations.
3) Limit applies to this account only and is explicitly tied to the pilot deal id BW-2027-391.

2) Add explicit non-precedent and no-modification-of-SLA clauses: the concession must not change standard SLOs beyond the pilot period and must not be cited by customer as a precedent for future negotiations.

If performance metrics deviate beyond 3x expected patterns we reserve the right to pause pilot or require mitigation.

I do not object to the 5% credit cap for monthly service credits during the pilot or the one-time 20% usage credit capped at $12k — both look contractable provided the clauses above are included.

I have attached the redline with tracked language and a short definition section. If Sales wants a faster route: I can pre-approve final language and ask Legal Ops to stamp and route for signoff after Finance confirms the cap.

Date: 2027-07-02T09:15:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Request: BrightWave pilot - temporary latency grace and pilot credit

Finance here — reviewed the numbers in pilot_pricing_sheet.xlsx. Approve the commercial concession under these constraints:
- 20% one-time pilot credit approved, capped at $12,000 total.
- Monthly service credit exposure during the pilot limited to 5% of that month’s invoice (cap is per-month, not cumulative).
- Discount must be booked as a contra-revenue promotion and cannot be applied to committed reserved capacity charges.

1) Pilot period: begins on execution of the attached amendment and runs exactly 90 calendar days.
2) Latency SLO: 99p tail latency tolerance increased to 600ms for the pilot period only.

Next steps: Sales Ops will prepare the DocuSign packet (amendment + commercial approval email + pricing sheet). Please sign the DocuSign packet to start the pilot. DocuSign packet: https://www.docusign.net/exec/packet/0a1b2c3d

Avery — please send the signed packet back to me and coordinate the kickoff.

Appreciate the team’s quick turnaround. Samir — looking forward to getting you up and running on 7/06 once signatures are in place.

Best,

title: RE: Post-term usage makeup and invoice amortization
preview:
From: Carlos Vega <carlos.vega@aurorahealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 30 Jun 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Post-term usage makeup — May/June spike and PO-8891

Hi Ben,

Quick one — during a DB migration in May we unexpectedly spiked above our committed capacity for about 8 days. Our engineering team attached actual token/embedding usage for May–June (see attached). Per our contract language it looks like that results in a retroactive usage adjustment.

Can Redwood either issue a one-time makeup invoice we can pay against PO-8891, or alternatively amortize that amount across our upcoming renewal (we're evaluating a capacity increase at renewal). Our finance team prefers an amortized approach if that's possible, but we understand there are accounting constraints.

Attachment: aurora-usage-may-jun.csv (csv)

Thanks — can you advise next steps and timelines? We need something in place before our July 15 renewal decision.

—Carlos Vega
CTO, Aurora Health Systems
car los.vega@aurorahealth.com | +1 (415) 555-0199

-----

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Carlos Vega <carlos.vega@aurorahealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 30 Jun 2027 11:03:14 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-term usage makeup — May/June spike and PO-8891

Carlos — thanks for the heads up and for attaching the usage export. Looping in Laura (Revenue Ops) and Monica (CS) to determine the cleanest path. Two pragmatic options we commonly use:

1) One-off invoice for the retroactive amount (explicit line item referencing PO-8891) — straightforward for accounting.
2) Amortize the retro amount across the renewal term (requires a renewal agreement amendment) — keeps cashflow smoother for customers but needs internal finance signoff.

If Aurora is planning to increase committed capacity at renewal, amortization tends to work well. Laura — can you confirm if Finance will allow the amortization for a case like this and whether we can offer a partial credit if Aurora signs a 12-month extension by July 15? I attached a draft invoice (for discussion) to show line items and amounts.

Attachments: amended-invoice-draft.pdf (pdf)

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 (650) 555-0133

-----

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Carlos Vega <carlos.vega@aurorahealth.com>
Date: Thu, 01 Jul 2027 09:45:26 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-term usage makeup — May/June spike and PO-8891

Ben / Monica — reviewed the usage CSV and numbers look valid. Per our billing policy we can do either path but with the following constraints: 

- One-off invoice (INV-2027-3491-ONEOFF.pdf) is easiest: it will be payable against PO-8891 and will not change renewal accounting.
- Amortization across the renewal requires a signed amendment that explicitly references the amortized amount and term. Finance requires the customer commit to the renewal (signed or DocuSigned) before we recognize the amortized amount in the renewal schedule.
- We can offer a 20% credit on the retroactive charge if Aurora signs a 12-month term by July 15. That credit is contingent on the signed renewal and listing PO-8891 on the invoice.

Ben — I'll prepare the one-off invoice template and the amendment language. Please confirm which path the customer prefers and I will fast-track the invoice.

Laura Bennett
Revenue Operations Manager | Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.com | +1 (650) 555-0177

Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 01 Jul 2027 15:20:05 -0700
Subject: RE: Post-term usage makeup — May/June spike and PO-8891

Carlos — thanks, appreciate the quick turnaround. To be explicit, here are the options and our recommended path: 

Option A — One-off invoice - Amount: $12,450 (per attached line items)
- Terms: Net 30, will reference PO-8891
- Timeline: Invoice issued within 2 business days upon confirmation

Option B — Amortize across renewal (recommended if you move forward with expansion)
- Amortized amount: $12,450
- Example amortization: $8,300 applied over 12 months at $691/mo (remaining $4,150 as payment now or credited per discussion)
- Conditional credit: 20% reduction on the retroactive charge if Aurora signs a 12-month renewal by July 15

If you prefer B, we'll prepare the renewal amendment and the amortization schedule; Finance requires a signed DocuSign before we book it that way. I've included a DocuSign link below for the amendment draft so you can review and sign.

DocuSign (amendment draft): https://www.docusign.com/link/renewal-DEAL-3491

Ben
