From: Procurement Team <procurement@harbortech.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Evan Li <evan.li@harbortech.com>
Date: Thu, 24 Jun 2027 08:12:00 -0700

Recap: 
- Pilot scope: 3 engineers, staging integration, 30-day pilot (logs + embeddings use).
- Pricing discussed: Starter reserved capacity + per-token overage (we documented in deck).

Asks / open items: 
1) Payment path: will this be a PO, corporate card, or Marketplace purchase? If PO, any internal thresholds/timing we should expect?
2) Budget owner / finance sponsor: who signs off on the budget and who will be the final signatory for contracting?
3) Procurement contacts & docs: does procurement need SOC2 / DPA / specific ISO evidence? Any preferred PO template or DocuSign requirement?

I attached a short mutual_action_plan and a one-page procurement checklist to make it easy for your team to pass along. If it's easiest we can loop in procurement and your finance lead on a short call tomorrow.

Next steps: confirm answers to 1–3 and I'll circulate a tailored MAP and sample SOW for the pilot.

> 2) Budget owner / finance sponsor: who signs off on the budget and who will be the final signatory for contracting?
> 3) Procurement contacts & docs: does procurement need SOC2 / DPA / specific ISO evidence? Any preferred PO template or DocuSign requirement?

Thanks Ben — great demo. Answers inline below: 

1) Payment path: For pilot <$5k we'll likely use a corporate card. Anything above ~$50k requires a PO + finance approval. Marketplace purchase is possible (we prefer that) but it still needs procurement sign-off; it speeds procurement in some cases but adds an internal catalog review.

2) Budget owner: Sophia Zhang (Dir, Data Engineering) is the economic owner and will greenlight the budget. Final signatory is typically our VP Finance, Rajiv Menon — he signs anything >$100k, but for PO-level deals the Finance sponsor (Rajiv) needs to be looped early.

3) Procurement needs: SOC2 Type II and a standard DPA. Procurement prefers our PO template (they'll send) and DocuSign for signatures. They also often request a short IP/data flow diagram for vendor onboarding.

I'll loop in procurement and Sophia here. Procurement's point: procurement@harbortech.com (Liam Ortiz).

title: Throughput handover and acceptance checklist
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Johnson <alex.johnson@nimble-retail.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 21 Jun 2027 15:02:00 -0700
Subject: Throughput handover and acceptance checklist

Hi Alex — thanks again for the call today. Quick recap and next steps so we have alignment for the trial: 

Summary of ask:
- NimbleRetail wants a 2-week trial to validate latency and cost under peak storefront traffic for search and recommendations.
- Primary success metrics: 95th pct latency < 220ms for read paths, < 350ms for embedding lookup + rerank; error rate < 0.5% on steady-state traffic.

Traffic profile we expect from you for the intake (can you confirm or correct?):
- Baseline sustained RPS: 120 rps (search endpoints)
- Peak bursts: up to 900 rps for 60–120s during promotional windows
- Avg token lengths: prompts ~ 45 tokens; responses ~ 80 tokens
- Typical concurrency: 18 clients simultaneously holding open chat/stream sessions (short-lived)

Immediate next steps (owner in parentheses):
1) Alex: share a recent representative traffic export (CSV) and a short list of top 10 endpoints and their weights. (Attachment preferred)
2) Marissa: produce a configuration proposal (baseline and burst profiles) and map to Redwood instance sizes; estimate token costs.
3) Ben: schedule a 60-min engineering handshake for Thurs to confirm authentication, test keys, and a first baseline run.

Attachments: poc-eval-template.xlsx (basic success metric workbook)

If the traffic numbers above differ from your expectations, reply inline with corrected values. Once we have the sample export, Marissa will draft the exact acceptance checklist and a timeline.

Thanks — Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai
[Attachment: poc-eval-template.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet, 48KB)]

From: Alex Johnson <alex.johnson@nimble-retail.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 21 Jun 2027 17:15:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Throughput handover and acceptance checklist

Ben — appreciate the clear summary. Confirming the majority of your numbers and attaching a small sample export: sample-traffic-export.csv. Inline clarifications below.

Traffic realities (confirmed):
- Sustained RPS: 100–130 rps for search + personalization (variable by hour). We see weekdays 09:00–12:00 peak.
- Promo bursts: historically up to 1k rps for 45–90s, especially on flash deals.
- Tokens: prompts ~ 30–70 tokens (search + user context), responses range 60–120 tokens (personalization + explanation).
- Concurrency: 12–25 depending on region; US west cluster usually 15–18.

Additional notes:
- We'll want to validate cold-start behavior and also reranking/embedding hot cache behavior.
- Security: we need to confirm SOC2 evidence before full-prod (happy to accept a scoping questionnaire).

Attached: sample-traffic-export.csv (first 2 hours from last Tuesday).

Question for Redwood: do you recommend enabling KV prefix caching for the rerank flows during the trial, or should we run a conservative config first and iterate?

Thanks — Alex
Alex Johnson
Head of Platform Engineering
NimbleRetail
alex.johnson@nimble-retail.com
[Attachment: sample-traffic-export.csv (text/csv, 92KB)]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Johnson <alex.johnson@nimble-retail.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 22 Jun 2027 09:48:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Throughput handover and acceptance checklist

Thanks Alex — the export is perfect and confirms the peak/burst patterns we discussed. Looping in Aditya (technical solutions lead) to own the handshake and baseline scheduling. Below is a concrete plan and owners.

Proposed POC timeline and owners:
- Day 0 (this week): Redwood provisions a test API key and a dedicated namespace; NimbleRetail provides service account + sample dataset. (Owner: Ben / Alex)
- Day 1–2: Baseline run at sustained RPS (100 rps) to validate latency and correctness. Collect token-level cost and 95/99 pctl latencies. (Owner: Aditya)
- Day 3–4: Burst rehearsal: run three scripted bursts that mimic your promo windows (600–1k rps for 60–90s). Measure tail latency and error behavior. (Owner: Aditya)
- Day 5–10: Stabilization and tuning: enable caching permutations if needed; compare quantization profiles; cost/perf readout. (Owner: Marissa)
- Day 11–14: Final acceptance run and readout; decision gate based on acceptance checklist. (Owner: Ben / Marissa)

Acceptance checklist (draft):
- Functional: all endpoints return correct responses under baseline load (sanity checks pass).
- Latency: 95th pct < 220ms (search), 95th pct < 350ms (embedding + rerank) during baseline.
- Burst resilience: system remains available and error rate < 1% during scripted bursts.
- Cost signals: per-token cost estimate within agreed threshold (TBD in final workbook).

On caching: start conservative (no cross-request KV caching) for baseline to capture cold-paths, then run an enabled-caching iteration to quantify improvement. Aditya — can you confirm the test key and a proposed time for the baseline run?

Thanks — Ben

Following our kickoff call for the SIG assessment of LumaPay, we need a compact bundle of artifacts to validate your secure development controls. Please provide the items below by COB Friday (23 Jun) if possible. If any of these require redaction or an alternate delivery mechanism, flag it in your reply.

Required artifacts (high priority):

title: Storage TTL & consistency runbook: Pinecone / pgvector / S3 sync
preview:
From: Elena Ruiz <elena@vectorops.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Jun 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Sync model & TTL approach for Pinecone <> S3 pilot

Hi Ben —

Thanks for the quick intro last week. VectorOps built a lightweight sync layer that keeps a managed vector index (e.g., Pinecone) warm while using S3 as the canonical cold store. We have a few open questions on TTL semantics and consistency for a pilot with Redwood:

- Where should TTL enforcement live (index vs. cold store vs. service layer)?
- How strict do you need read-after-write guarantees for fresh embeddings?
- Is cross-region replication required for the pilot, or can we scope it to a single region?

Attached a short proposal (vectorops-ttl-proposal.pdf) that lays out two models: write-through (sync to index then async to S3) and write-to-canonical (S3 first, index warmed lazily).

We’d love to set a 30–45 minute tech sync with whoever owns index/fallback at Redwood. Suggested times Wed/Thu mornings PT.

Best,
Elena Ruiz
Head of Integrations | VectorOps Inc.
elena@vectorops.com
+1 (415) 555-0182

Attachment: vectorops-ttl-proposal.pdf (PDF, 240 KB)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena@vectorops.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Jun 2027 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Sync model & TTL approach for Pinecone <> S3 pilot

Elena —

Thanks for the proposal and the clear models. I can run the tech sync; looping in Karthik (index/runtime) so he can bring feasibility notes. Quick answers up-front:

- Prefer write-through for initial pilot (index first, async persist to S3) so we can meet low-latency read SLAs.
- Read-after-write: soft R-A-W is acceptable (seconds), but we need guarantees that the index will not serve stale vectors beyond configured TTL.
- Start single-region (us-west-2) for MVP; we can layer cross-region replication in phase 2.

For the sync call, can you confirm: expected QPS, typical document size (avg tokens per embedding), and dataset footprint? Also please share any schema/config examples (TTL granularity, per-namespace policies). I’ll bring a sample config we use internally (pilot-config-sample.yaml).

Proposed agenda for the call:
1) Quick intro & success criteria (5m)
2) Expected workload & constraints (10m)
3) TTL enforcement patterns + failure modes (15m)
4) Rollout & monitoring (10m)
5) Next steps & owners (5m)

If Wed/Thu morning PT still works, send a calendar invite and I’ll add Karthik.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Sr. Partnerships Manager | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com

Attachment: pilot-config-sample.yaml (YAML, 1.2 KB)

From: Elena Ruiz <elena@vectorops.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Samir Patel <samir@pinecone.io>
Date: Wed, 16 Jun 2027 08:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Sync model & TTL approach for Pinecone <> S3 pilot

Ben / Karthik / Samir —

Thanks for looping people in. A few quick data points for the call:
- Expected steady-state QPS for MVP: ~300 qps (spiky +2x during peak search windows).
- Typical embedding size: 768 dims (float32); per-doc payload ~3 KB.
- Current customer footprint for cold store: ~40 TB (S3), retention target 90 days.
- We enforce TTL per-namespace; TTL granularity is seconds.

We prefer the write-through model for low-latency UX. Our implementation uses an append-only write log to ensure idempotent retries and an async compactor that writes snapshots to S3 once per hour. On eviction, we do a coordinated delete: remove index entry, mark tombstone in S3 metadata and compact in next snapshot window. See attached short design and a link to the architecture diagram: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3c-vectorops-pilot

Happy to demo the compactor flow on the call. Proposed times: Thu 2027-06-17 09:30 PT or Thu 2027-06-17 11:30 PT. Which works?

—Elena
Elena Ruiz
Head of Integrations | VectorOps Inc.

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 22 Jun 2027 15:33:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Sync model & TTL approach for Pinecone <> S3 pilot

Ben / Laura —

Read the proposal and had a short technical pass. High-level notes and recommended pilot pattern:

1) Pilot model (recommendation): write-through for 1.0. Implementation:
   - Primary write: index (Pinecone) with ack back to client.
   - Async persist: append-only write log -> hourly snapshot to S3 (canonical).
   - Tombstones: immediate index delete + tombstone write to log; compacter removes payload from S3 on 2x TTL grace (configurable).

2) Consistency guarantees:
   - Provide soft R-A-W for 99% of ops (<= 3s).
   - Stronger R-A-W for critical paths can be supported by blocking until snapshot completes (not recommended for MVP).

3) pgvector fallback:
   - pgvector can act as a regional fallback for local reads. We should surface a header (x-rr-fallback: pgvector) for reads that miss index. That avoids synchronous reads to S3.

4) Monitoring & ops:
   - Add metrics: write-log-lag_seconds, compactor-last-success, tombstone-count, s3-snapshot-size.
   - Playbook: auto-retry logic on failed snapshots; manual rollback steps documented in snapshot-runbook-draft.md.

5) Infra/effort estimate (back-of-envelope):
   - Index hooks + write-log: 2 engineer-weeks (one engineer) to implement idempotent logging and tombstone writes.
   - Compactor & snapshot infra: 3 engineer-weeks (can be shared with VectorOps code).
   - Ops dashboards & alerts: 1 engineer-week + on-call runbook.

6) Proposed pilot scope & timeline:
   - Week 0: finalize contract & success criteria (throughput, R-A-W SLO, retention).
   - Week 1–2: implement index hooks + small test harness (staging).
   - Week 3: integrated demo with VectorOps (staging).
   - Week 4–6: limited production pilot (single tenant, us-west-2).

- Ben: confirm success criteria & get VectorOps to sign off on the proposed scope.
- Laura: draft monitoring/retention SLA notes + confirm 90-day logging feasibility.
- Karthik: start a branch for idempotent write-log and share initial PR by EOD next week.

I attached a small runbook and a sample config for the compactor flow. If everyone agrees, we can bring Pinecone’s engineering (Samir) into the call for index behavior specifics.

-Karthik

text:
From: Elena Ruiz <elena@vectorops.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Jun 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Sync model & TTL approach for Pinecone <> S3 pilot

Hi Ben —

Thanks for the quick intro last week. VectorOps built a lightweight sync layer that keeps a managed vector index (e.g., Pinecone) warm while using S3 as the canonical cold store. We have a few open questions on TTL semantics and consistency for a pilot with Redwood:

- Where should TTL enforcement live (index vs. cold store vs. service layer)?
- How strict do you need read-after-write guarantees for fresh embeddings?
- Is cross-region replication required for the pilot, or can we scope it to a single region?

Attached a short proposal (vectorops-ttl-proposal.pdf) that lays out two models: write-through (sync to index then async to S3) and write-to-canonical (S3 first, index warmed lazily).

We’d love to set a 30–45 minute tech sync with whoever owns index/fallback at Redwood. Suggested times Wed/Thu mornings PT.

Best,
Elena Ruiz
Head of Integrations | VectorOps Inc.
elena@vectorops.com
+1 (415) 555-0182

Attachment: vectorops-ttl-proposal.pdf (PDF, 240 KB)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena@vectorops.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Jun 2027 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Sync model & TTL approach for Pinecone <> S3 pilot

Elena —

Thanks for the proposal and the clear models. I can run the tech sync; looping in Karthik (index/runtime) so he can bring feasibility notes. Quick answers up-front:

- Prefer write-through for initial pilot (index first, async persist to S3) so we can meet low-latency read SLAs.
- Read-after-write: soft R-A-W is acceptable (seconds), but we need guarantees that the index will not serve stale vectors beyond configured TTL.
- Start single-region (us-west-2) for MVP; we can layer cross-region replication in phase 2.

For the sync call, can you confirm: expected QPS, typical document size (avg tokens per embedding), and dataset footprint? Also please share any schema/config examples (TTL granularity, per-namespace policies). I’ll bring a sample config we use internally (pilot-config-sample.yaml).

Proposed agenda for the call:
1) Quick intro & success criteria (5m)
2) Expected workload & constraints (10m)
3) TTL enforcement patterns + failure modes (15m)
4) Rollout & monitoring (10m)
5) Next steps & owners (5m)

If Wed/Thu morning PT still works, send a calendar invite and I’ll add Karthik.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Sr. Partnerships Manager | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com

Attachment: pilot-config-sample.yaml (YAML, 1.2 KB)

From: Elena Ruiz <elena@vectorops.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Samir Patel <samir@pinecone.io>
Date: Wed, 16 Jun 2027 08:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Sync model & TTL approach for Pinecone <> S3 pilot

Ben / Karthik / Samir —

Thanks for looping people in. A few quick data points for the call:
- Expected steady-state QPS for MVP: ~300 qps (spiky +2x during peak search windows).
- Typical embedding size: 768 dims (float32); per-doc payload ~3 KB.
- Current customer footprint for cold store: ~40 TB (S3), retention target 90 days.
- We enforce TTL per-namespace; TTL granularity is seconds.

We prefer the write-through model for low-latency UX. Our implementation uses an append-only write log to ensure idempotent retries and an async compactor that writes snapshots to S3 once per hour. On eviction, we do a coordinated delete: remove index entry, mark tombstone in S3 metadata and compact in next snapshot window. See attached short design and a link to the architecture diagram: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3c-vectorops-pilot

Happy to demo the compactor flow on the call. Proposed times: Thu 2027-06-17 09:30 PT or Thu 2027-06-17 11:30 PT. Which works?

—Elena

From: Elena Ruiz <elena@svbbeacon.com>

Subject: Request: segmented remit + monthly bridge for enterprise pilot

Hi Alex,

Thanks again for the demo last week — SVB Beacon is excited to move into a paid pilot for Q3. A couple of billing constraints from our finance team before we finalize:

- We need invoices split by legal entity (US operations vs EU subsidiary) for tax and local payment routing.
- For Q3 only, we’d like to remain on quarterly billing cadence, but we need a short-term monthly \"bridge\" invoice for the first two months while our internal accounting cutovers finish.
- Payment terms: we can agree to Net-90 on the quarterly invoices, provided you can issue the monthly bridge invoices with Net-30.

Can Redwood do segmented remit (two invoices per quarter) plus the two monthly bridge invoices? If so we’ll provide a PO and billing contacts.

Thanks,
Elena Ruiz

Subject: Re: Request: segmented remit + monthly bridge for enterprise pilot

Elena — really appreciate the detail and the PO. We can support segmented remit and a temporary monthly bridge, but this configuration requires Deal Desk and Finance signoff because it mixes multiple invoice rhythms and different payment terms across invoices.

Immediate next steps I propose:
1) I’ll escalate to Deal Desk / Finance for conditional approval and provide an invoice amendment draft.
2) Karthik, can you advise on risk and whether we require an advance deposit to mitigate AR exposure?
3) If Finance is comfortable, Legal will need to add a short amendment describing the segmented remit and the bridge invoices (minimal redline).

Elena — can you confirm the PO number in the attachment and the two billing entities (US / EU) to put on the invoices? Also confirm the billing contacts for accounts payable.

Alex Martinez
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 21 Jun 2027 09:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: segmented remit + monthly bridge for enterprise pilot

Alex / Grace — reviewed SVB Beacon’s ask. Key points from Finance:

- Segmented remit (separate legal-entity invoices) is operationally fine but needs clear mapping in our billing system and the amendment to avoid double-counting usage.
- Bridge invoices increase AR risk if quarterly invoices are Net-90. We can approve a conditional plan if SVB Beacon provides an upfront bridge deposit equal to 30% of the expected quarter value, to be applied against the first quarterly invoice.
- Alternatively, we can make the bridge invoices Net-30 (as requested) with a credit hold clause that puts the account on temporary restrictions if any bridge invoice is late by 15 days.

I prefer the deposit path (30%) for lower credit risk. Marcus — please draft minimal amendment language for segmented remit + deposit clause.

Karthik
Head of Finance, Redwood Inference

To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>

title: Supplier enrollment: PO field mapping, invoice delivery & encryption requirements
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sophie Taylor <sophie.taylor@orpheushealth.com>, Orpheus Procurement <procurement@orpheushealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 18 Jun 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Supplier enrollment: PO field mapping, invoice delivery & encryption requirements

Hi Sophie / Procurement team,

Thanks for the quick intro—happy to get Redwood set up in Orpheus's vendor registry. To avoid back-and-forth during our onboarding, attaching our AP_Onboarding_Packet_v2.pdf and a populated invoice_template_Orpheus.xlsx so you can see our preferred fields. A few asks up front so we can align with your AP system: 

- Required PO fields: we need to know the exact PO identifier you issue (PO#, order_line_id if line-level matching is required, and whether unit_price/unit_qty are included on the PO).
- PO delivery method: do you send POs via email, SFTP drop, or EDI/Peppol? If you support push webhooks for PO acknowledgements, please share the endpoint or schema.
- Vendor token: if Orpheus issues a vendor token for API/portal lookups, please advise the token format & the PR timeframe to request it.
- Invoice delivery: does AP require invoices to be sent to invoices@orpheushealth.com, or do you prefer SFTP/AS2/PEPPOL? Any encryption requirements (PGP key or TLS-only)?
- Tax/Vendor evidence: confirm whether you need a W9 (US) or any VAT documentation; we can provide a signed W9 and our tax contact.
- Payment terms & remit: please confirm standard payment terms (Net 30/45/60), remit-to address, and whether early-pay programs require special invoice flags.

If you can reply with a short checklist (preferred file format and required fields) I’ll get our ops and finance team to implement it this week. Proposed next steps: once we have your required fields and remit profile, Laura will validate the invoice_template and Grace will schedule a 30-min handoff call.

Thanks,

Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0146
Redwood Inference

Attachments: AP_Onboarding_Packet_v2.pdf (application/pdf), invoice_template_Orpheus.xlsx (spreadsheet)

From: Sophie Taylor <sophie.taylor@orpheushealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 18 Jun 2027 11:03:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Supplier enrollment: PO field mapping, invoice delivery & encryption requirements

Hi Ben / team,

Thanks — this is very thorough and exactly the level of detail we need. Quick answers below and I’ve attached our vendor_enrollment_form.pdf for the legal entity details.

- PO fields: Orpheus issues PONumber (string) and LineNumber (optional) for line-level matching. We do not send unit_price on the PO for services, but we do include POLineAmounts for product orders when applicable. Your invoices should include PONumber in the invoice header and, if you are billing multiple lines, repeat LineNumber with matching amounts.
- PO delivery: POs are generated from our procurement portal and emailed to suppliers at the address on file. We can also provide a vendor_token if you register in our portal (recommended). No EDI for initial enrollments.
- Vendor token & portal: after you submit the vendor_enrollment_form.pdf, our vendor portal will create a vendor_token (format: VT-XXXXX) and send a confirmation. Use that token for PO lookups.
- Invoice delivery & encryption: Preferred method is SFTP to sftp.orpheushealth.com (invoices/ drop). If you must send by email, invoices must be encrypted with our PGP key (attached: Orpheus_PGP_Public.asc).
- Tax docs: For US entities we require a completed W9; for non-US suppliers we need a W-8BEN or local tax certificate.
- Payment terms: Standard Net 60. We do have an early-pay program (1.5% fee for Net 15) — opt-in flag is \"EARLYPAY\" on the invoice line.

We can provision portal access within 24-48 hours once the vendor_enrollment_form and W9 are received. Attaching a sample PO and our vendor_portal_instructions.pdf.

--
Sophie Taylor
Procurement Manager, Orpheus Health
Sophie.Taylor@orpheushealth.com

Attachments: vendor_enrollment_form.pdf (pdf), Orpheus_PGP_Public.asc (pgp public key), sample_PO_45722.pdf (pdf), vendor_portal_instructions.pdf (pdf)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sophie Taylor <sophie.taylor@orpheushealth.com>, Orpheus Procurement <procurement@orpheushealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 21 Jun 2027 08:21:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Supplier enrollment: PO field mapping, invoice delivery & encryption requirements

Hi Sophie — perfect, that helps a lot. A couple quick clarifications before we submit the enrollment form: 

1) SFTP details: do you require a specific directory structure (e.g., /invoices/Redwood/) or will the drop be keyed by vendor_token once it's provisioned?
2) PO changes: If a PO comes without LineNumber, should we attach a single invoice referencing the top-level PONumber and list services as a single line item?
3) Early-pay flag: please confirm whether \"EARLYPAY\" needs to be in the invoice header or on each invoice line for automated processing.

We are attaching our signed W9 (W9_BenCarter_2027.pdf) and a completed vendor_enrollment_form (populated). Laura — can you validate the invoice_template_Orpheus.xlsx against their PO/NLine expectations? Grace — once Sophie confirms the SFTP directory we should schedule a 20-min integration call (I suggest Wed 6/23).

I'll send the completed enrollment in this thread once I have the answers.

Thanks,
Ben

Attachments: W9_BenCarter_2027.pdf (pdf), vendor_enrollment_form_filled.pdf (pdf), invoice_template_Orpheus.xlsx (spreadsheet)

From: Orpheus Procurement <procurement@orpheushealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 21 Jun 2027 12:09:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Supplier enrollment: PO field mapping, invoice delivery & encryption requirements

Hi Ben / team,

Thanks for the form and W9 — portal access has been provisioned. Details below: 

- Vendor token: VT-94821 (use this in the invoice file name or SFTP path).
- SFTP: Please drop invoices at /invoices/VT-94821/ on sftp.orpheushealth.com. We will pick up files hourly.
- PO handling: If there is no LineNumber on the PO, invoice at the PO level (single line). If there are multiple billable rows, include LineNumber and amounts per line.
- EARLYPAY: place the EARLYPAY flag in the invoice header field \"PaymentFlags\"; our intake will act on header flags for the early-pay program.
- Onboarding call: Scheduled for Wed, Jun 23 2027 at 10:00 AM ET. Meeting invite sent (please accept).

Attached: PO_45722.pdf (this will be the first PO you'll see). Please upload a PO acknowledgement file to /acks/VT-94821/ after you ingest the PO (a simple JSON or PDF with the PONumber and acknowledgement timestamp is fine).

Regards,
Procurement Ops — Orpheus Health
procurement@orpheushealth.com

Subject: Supplier enrollment: PO field mapping, invoice delivery & encryption requirements

Hi Sophie / Procurement team,

Thanks for the quick intro—happy to get Redwood set up in Orpheus's vendor registry. To avoid back-and-forth during our onboarding, attaching our AP_Onboarding_Packet_v2.pdf and a populated invoice_template_Orpheus.xlsx so you can see our preferred fields. A few asks up front so we can align with your AP system: 

- Required PO fields: we need to know the exact PO identifier you issue (PO#, order_line_id if line-level matching is required, and whether unit_price/unit_qty are included on the PO).
- PO delivery method: do you send POs via email, SFTP drop, or EDI/Peppol? If you support push webhooks for PO acknowledgements, please share the endpoint or schema.
- Vendor token: if Orpheus issues a vendor token for API/portal lookups, please advise the token format & the PR timeframe to request it.
- Invoice delivery: does AP require invoices to be sent to invoices@orpheushealth.com, or do you prefer SFTP/AS2/PEPPOL? Any encryption requirements (PGP key or TLS-only)?
- Tax/Vendor evidence: confirm whether you need a W9 (US) or any VAT documentation; we can provide a signed W9 and our tax contact.
- Payment terms & remit: please confirm standard payment terms (Net 30/45/60), remit-to address, and whether early-pay programs require special invoice flags.

If you can reply with a short checklist (preferred file format and required fields) I’ll get our ops and finance team to implement it this week. Proposed next steps: once we have your required fields and remit profile, Laura will validate the invoice_template and Grace will schedule a 30-min handoff call.

Thanks,

Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0146
Redwood Inference

Attachments: AP_Onboarding_Packet_v2.pdf (application/pdf), invoice_template_Orpheus.xlsx (spreadsheet)

From: Sophie Taylor <sophie.taylor@orpheushealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 18 Jun 2027 11:03:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Supplier enrollment: PO field mapping, invoice delivery & encryption requirements

Hi Ben / team,

Thanks — this is very thorough and exactly the level of detail we need. Quick answers below and I’ve attached our vendor_enrollment_form.pdf for the legal entity details.

- PO fields: Orpheus issues PONumber (string) and LineNumber (optional) for line-level matching. We do not send unit_price on the PO for services, but we do include POLineAmounts for product orders when applicable. Your invoices should include PONumber in the invoice header and, if you are billing multiple lines, repeat LineNumber with matching amounts.
- PO delivery: POs are generated from our procurement portal and emailed to suppliers at the address on file. We can also provide a vendor_token if you register in our portal (recommended). No EDI for initial enrollments.
- Vendor token & portal: after you submit the vendor_enrollment_form.pdf, our vendor portal will create a vendor_token (format: VT-XXXXX) and send a confirmation. Use that token for PO lookups.
- Invoice delivery & encryption: Preferred method is SFTP to sftp.orpheushealth.com (invoices/ drop). If you must send by email, invoices must be encrypted with our PGP key (attached: Orpheus_PGP_Public.asc).
- Tax docs: For US entities we require a completed W9; for non-US suppliers we need a W-8BEN or local tax certificate.
- Payment terms: Standard Net 60. We do have an early-pay program (1.5% fee for Net 15) — opt-in flag is \"EARLYPAY\" on the invoice line.

We can provision portal access within 24-48 hours once the vendor_enrollment_form and W9 are received. Attaching a sample PO and our vendor_portal_instructions.pdf.

--
Sophie Taylor
Procurement Manager, Orpheus Health
Sophie.Taylor@orpheushealth.com

Attachments: vendor_enrollment_form.pdf (pdf), Orpheus_PGP_Public.asc (pgp public key), sample_PO_45722.pdf (pdf), vendor_portal_instructions.pdf (pdf)

title: Marketplace onboarding: co-sell eligibility, SKU gating and per-cloud packaging
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Morales <elena.morales@cloudbridge.com>, aws-partner-manager@amazon.com
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 18 Jun 2027 09:13:00 -0700
Subject: Marketplace onboarding: co-sell eligibility, SKU gating and per-cloud packaging

Hi Elena / AM / team,

Thanks again for the sync this morning. Per our conversation I pulled together the initial decision areas we need to finalize so CloudBridge and Redwood can go live across AWS, Azure, and GCP marketplaces while keeping co-sell eligibility intact. High-level asks below — please review and flag anything missing.

Key decision areas (summary):
- SKU variants: standard / enterprise / dedicated (what maps to Reserved/Dedicated offering).
- Region gating: US-first rollout, EU parity for data residency, APAC selective (Japan, Australia) depending on legal/compliance readiness.
- Billing type differences: AWS/Marketplace Metering (usage + subscription), Azure (SaaS offer + commercial marketplace), GCP (SaaS + private catalog).
- Co-sell eligibility gating: minimum MRR in marketplace, partner co-sell registration, and joint-field enablement artifacts (pitch deck + one-pager + architecture diagram).

Action items for our team:
1) CloudBridge to confirm which SKU names you prefer exposed in each storefront (we recommend short names: RBW-Standard, RBW-Enterprise, RBW-Dedicated).
2) Redwood to produce technical listing artifacts (SLA, architecture diagram, deployment variants) and a short co-sell one-pager — attached draft.
3) Agree on timeline/priority per cloud: AWS + Azure priority week of 6/28, GCP targeted 7/12 pending GCP listing constraints.

Attachment: RBW_marketplace_sku_matrix_v1.xlsx (draft SKU matrix + recommended region gating).

Next step: Elena — can you confirm if CloudBridge needs a single SKU per offer (CloudBridge has legacy billing constraints), or if you can accept per-cloud variantization? If single SKU required, we’ll propose a mapping layer that extracts the correct marketplace attributes on checkout.

Thanks — Ben

--
Ben Carter
Senior Partnerships Manager, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai
Redwood Inference


From: Elena Morales <elena.morales@cloudbridge.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 18 Jun 2027 12:05:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Marketplace onboarding: co-sell eligibility, SKU gating and per-cloud packaging

Ben — thanks for the deck and matrix. Quick thoughts from CloudBridge: we would prefer to present a single canonical SKU on our side for legacy billing reasons, but we can accept per-cloud variants if Redwood handles the reconciliation (pricing/attributes mapping) and ensures invoices align.

Questions from our compliance/ops lead:
- For EU parity: will data residency be explicit per listing or handled via regional endpoints?
- For co-sell, do we need any special paperwork to qualify for AWS co-sell (ISV Accelerate) beyond what you mentioned?

We can accept your proposed timeline as long as we don't have to rework legal concurrently — can we keep legal pass-through minimal?

Elena
Head of Platform Partnerships, CloudBridge
elena.morales@cloudbridge.com

---
On Fri, Jun 18, 2027 at 09:13 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai> wrote:
> (original message)


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Morales <elena.morales@cloudbridge.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 21 Jun 2027 08:42:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Marketplace onboarding: co-sell eligibility, SKU gating and per-cloud packaging

Elena — quick technical clarifications from the Redwood side:

1) Data residency / listings: we typically surface region-level notes in the listing and have regional endpoints for EU. For Azure we can request data residency flags as part of the offer assets; for AWS we recommend replicating the EU endpoint and explicitly stating \"EU-hosted instances for EU customers\" in the listing text.

2) Co-sell paperwork: AWS ISV Accelerate requires joint GTM registration and an enablement packet (customer reference + one-pager + joint sales play). We will own the enablement packet and provide the customer reference and deck. For Azure, the co-sell model is similar but requires validation by the Azure Marketplace team; the marketplace ops contact CC'd can confirm exact docs.

3) SKU variantization approach: we propose:
- SKU level names: RBW-STD (metered), RBW-ENT (subscription + metered add-on), RBW-DED (reserved/dedicated).
- Cloud-specific attributes (billing, metering) will be mapped in an internal reconciliation table. That lets CloudBridge keep a single canonical SKU externally while marketplaces show variants.

4) Gating thresholds: to preserve co-sell eligibility and avoid marketplace delist risk, we recommend:
- US rollout: all SKUs live if joint enablement packet provided.
- EU rollout: require data processing addendum in place + regional endpoint.
- APAC: gated to top-tier accounts initially; scaling requires APAC legal OK.

Attach: CoSell_Playbook_OVERVIEW.pdf (draft).

If that approach works, we should schedule a 30-min ops alignment this week to lock mapping and owners. I can take owner for the SKU mapping table.

Thanks,
Karthik
Head of Marketplace Ops, Redwood Inference


From: Elena Morales <elena.morales@cloudbridge.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 21 Jun 2027 11:37:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Marketplace onboarding: co-sell eligibility, SKU gating and per-cloud packaging

Thanks Karthik — that mapping approach sounds reasonable. A couple clarifications:
- CloudBridge will commit to providing one customer reference and a short joint case study within 30 days of go-live (for AWS co-sell).
- Regarding billing mapping: can Redwood provide a reconciliation cadence? Monthly is fine for us but we need the invoice to reflect customer-facing SKU.

We can meet Wed 6/23 10am ET for the ops alignment — Ben, Karthik, Soojin — does that work?

Best,
Elena

> On Mon, Jun 21, 2027 at 08:42 AM Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai> wrote:
> (original message)


From: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Morales <elena.morales@cloudbridge.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 21 Jun 2027 15:42:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Marketplace onboarding: co-sell eligibility, SKU gating and per-cloud packaging

Perfect — Wed 6/23 10am ET works for Redwood. Agenda items I'll bring to the ops alignment:
1) Final SKU mapping table (owner: Karthik) — confirm canonical SKU -> per-cloud variant mapping and prices.
2) Listing artifacts checklist (owner: Soojin) — architecture diagram, SLA statement, data residency note, co-sell one-pager.
3) Legal/finance gating (owner: Jonas) — confirm DPA status for EU and APAC gating rules.
4) Go-live schedule and rollback plan — target AWS/Azure week of 6/28, GCP 7/12, with rollback window first 72 hours post-listing.

Please review the attached SKU matrix before the call — I updated pricing bands slightly to reflect marketplace fees and co-sell margins. We should decide whether to keep the RBW-DED SKU visible on marketplace or to surface it only via private offers (recommended for Dedicated/reserved capacity).

See you Wed — Soojin
Senior Product Operations Manager, Redwood Inference
